name: Wyrope Williamsport
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1957 serving South Williamsport, PA with $41.5M in assets and 4,800+ members. Offers checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long: Wyrope Williamsport Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in South Williamsport, Pennsylvania. As a member-owned institution, it operates under the federal credit union charter and serves a specific geographic community in central Pennsylvania. The credit union has grown to steward $41.5 million in assets and maintain a membership base of over 4,800 loyal members. Wyrope Williamsport offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking and lending products. Members can open regular savings accounts, share draft (checking) accounts with 2,300+ active accounts, share certificates (CDs) with 100+ holders, and money market accounts. On the lending side, the credit union provides auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, mortgages (including 15-year fixed options), and credit cards. Digital services include online banking, mobile app access (iOS and Android), bill pay, electronic statement delivery, remote deposit capture, and mobile payments. As a community-focused credit union with a single branch and 1 ATM location, Wyrope Williamsport distinguishes itself through member-centric service and local commitment. The institution employs over 8 dedicated staff members and provides financial counseling, financial education programs, and student scholarships. Their digital infrastructure is actively maintained, with online banking and mobile app functionality reported as online and operational, plus a service status monitoring system for members to report issues. The primary limitation is geographic and membership-based access—this is a small, single-branch credit union serving a specific community in South Williamsport rather than a regional or national institution. The lobby was reported as currently closed at the time of this profile's source data. With $41.5M in assets and 4,800 members, it is a modest-sized credit union best suited for local residents and those within its membership eligibility criteria rather than members seeking extensive branch networks or specialized services.
pros: Member-owned credit union with 67+ years of established community presence since 1957
Competitive lending products including auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and credit cards at member rates
Comprehensive digital banking: online banking, mobile app (iOS/Android), bill pay, remote deposit capture, and mobile payments
Financial counseling and educational programs, plus student scholarship offerings
Modest asset base ($41.5M) allows personalized member service in tight-knit community
Share certificate (CD) and money market options for savings-focused members
Active service status monitoring with member outage reporting capability
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside South Williamsport area
Only 1 ATM limits convenient cash access compared to larger credit unions or banks
Lobby reported as currently closed, potentially reducing in-person service availability
Small institution (4,800 members, $41.5M assets) may have limited product customization or specialized services
Membership eligibility restricted to specific geographic community, not open to all
best_for: Local South Williamsport residents seeking community-based credit union membership with personalized service
Members wanting auto loans, mortgages, or personal loans at member-friendly rates without national bank bureaucracy
Savers and CD holders looking for federally-insured deposits with member ownership benefits
Financially-conscious individuals seeking financial counseling and education programs alongside basic banking
diagnosis: Wyrope Williamsport FCU is best for South Williamsport residents and those within its membership eligibility area who value community-focused credit union banking with member-owned governance and personalized service. The primary caveat is its single-branch, single-ATM footprint and membership-restricted access—this is not a solution for geographically dispersed consumers or those outside the membership criteria seeking nationwide banking options.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
